message:
fix(cli,server,tools,guard,storage,db): harden edge cases across multiple subsystems

CLI:
- Setup command output now renders as a styled box-drawing table in TTY
  mode with per-outcome icons and colors, falling back to plain text for
  non-TTY (pipelines, CI)
- Robot mode: use try_into().unwrap_or(i64::MAX) for limit casts instead
  of bare `as i64` to prevent silent truncation of large usize values

Server:
- Cleanup filesystem activity check now uses `git ls-files` fast path
  for glob patterns, avoiding catastrophic O(n) directory traversal
  through target/ and node_modules/; fallback glob capped at 5000 entries
- TUI toast focus index clamped on tick so dismissing the last toast
  doesn't leave a stale index pointing past visible notifications
- Message browser: handle empty `to` field by falling back to cc
  recipients or "undisclosed" in send confirmation summary

Database:
- mark_message_read / acknowledge_message: properly rollback and return
  error on Err outcome instead of silently using `now` timestamp (was
  swallowing the error and proceeding with stale data)

Guard:
- parse_name_status_z: unknown git status codes now capture their
  associated path and advance the parser by 2, maintaining alignment
  with subsequent entries (previously skipped the status but left the
  path dangling, causing offset drift)

Storage:
- FileLock retry backoff: center jitter around base_ms instead of
  always adding to it, producing more uniform spread and preventing
  consistently overshooting the intended delay

Tools:
- Build slots: clean up malformed lease files on discovery instead of
  silently skipping them; renew_build_slot skips already-released
  leases; release_build_slot is idempotent for already-released leases
- Contact parsing: trim whitespace from project slug in URI format
- Inbox fetch: allow limit=0 to reach the explicit error path instead
  of silently defaulting to 20
- Search: raise planner limit cap from 1000 to 10000 for large mailboxes

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
